entries.title: COMMENTARY ON PAUL'S EPISTLES on second-corinthians 13:13
entries.excerpt: Paul is calling the Corinthians to holiness, so that they may be bold enough to return the greeting of the saints. For they are greeted by the saints with the intention that they should imitate them.

entries.title: Homily 30 on 2 Corinthians on second-corinthians 13:13
entries.excerpt: And having said, "Salute one another with an holy kiss," he added, "All the saints salute you." By this also giving them good hopes. He has added this in the place of the kiss, knitting them together by the salutation, for the words also proceed from the same mouth from which the kiss. Seest thou how he brings them all together, both those who are widely separated in the body and those who are near, these by the kiss and those by the written message?

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE SECOND EPISTLE TO THE CORINTHIANS 13 on second-corinthians 13:13
entries.excerpt: All the saints sent their greetings, not just the leaders.

entries.title: Commentary on 2 Corinthians on second-corinthians 13:13
entries.excerpt: The present he unites with a kiss, the absent with a greeting, but even here the lips are joined, because the greeting proceeds from them.

entries.title: Commentary on 2 Corinthians on second-corinthians 13:13
entries.excerpt: He greets them on the part of the others when he says, all the saints greet you, because all the saints and faithful hope and desire and procure our salvation with their prayers. Hence, all of Christ's faithful hope and desire salvation for one another: "Make me a partaker, O God" (Ps. 119, Aquinas Latin).
